title: Billionaire Bill Gates Has 78% of His Foundation's $34 Billion Portfolio Invested in 4 Fantastic Stocks
author: Adam Levy
description: The Gates Foundation's $34 billion equity portfolio is heavily concentrated in four non-tech stocks: Berkshire Hathaway, Caterpillar, Waste Management, and Canadian National Railway. These investments reflect Warren Buffett's influence on Gates' strategy, favoring stable, predictable businesses in finance, industrials, and utilities over high-flying tech stocks. Recent data center demand has boosted Caterpillar to the portfolio's second-largest position.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Largest holding in the portfolio with strong operating results across core segments. New CEO Greg Abel is deploying capital effectively and improving margins. Trading at reasonable 1.46 price-to-book ratio.

Largest holding in the portfolio with strong operating results across core segments. New CEO Greg Abel is deploying capital effectively and improving margins. Trading at reasonable 1.46 price-to-book ratio.

Second-largest position benefiting from data center buildouts with 48% power generation revenue growth. However, stock trades at 40x earnings expectations, which may be difficult to justify despite margin expansion potential. Vulnerable to economic downturns.

Long-term holding with competitive advantages from landfill ownership and vertical integration. Recession-proof business with steady margin expansion potential. Fair valuation at 13x EV/EBITDA with growing ancillary businesses like medical waste solutions.

Efficient operator with high barriers to entry and recession-resistant pricing. Trading at discount (13.5x EV/EBITDA) relative to peers. Management focused on capital efficiency and free cash flow growth with shareholder returns through buybacks.

title: Chevron’s Microsoft Deal Turns Natural Gas Into an AI Trade
author: Jeffrey Neal Johnson
description: Chevron and Microsoft announced a 20-year, 2.67 gigawatt natural gas power purchase agreement (Project Kilby) to power a dedicated AI data center campus in Texas. The deal allows Chevron to monetize stranded natural gas from its Permian Basin operations while providing Microsoft with reliable baseload power independent of the public grid. The $7 billion project targets mid-teen IRR and represents a strategic shift for traditional energy producers to capitalize on AI infrastructure demand.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The deal converts stranded natural gas liabilities into a premium 20-year fixed revenue stream with mid-teen IRR. Chevron's strong financial position (fortress balance sheet, 38-year dividend streak, recent Hess acquisition) supports funding this strategic pivot. The company gains exposure to secular AI growth while solving regional gas takeaway constraints.

Secures dedicated, reliable 2.67 GW baseload power for AI data center operations, bypassing grid congestion and volatile commercial pricing. The off-grid design insulates Microsoft from Texas grid strain and multi-year interconnection queues affecting competitors.

Selected as a key infrastructure partner to provide heavy machinery and turbine expertise for the $7 billion Project Kilby construction, positioning the company to benefit from the growing AI data center power infrastructure buildout.

Its Solar Turbines subsidiary is tapped for turbine expertise and infrastructure construction on Project Kilby, providing revenue opportunity from the large-scale AI data center power generation project.

title: Why Has Caterpillar Stock Soared 184% in the Last 12 Months?
author: Eric Volkman
description: Caterpillar's stock has surged 184% over the past year, significantly outpacing the S&P 500's 25% gain. The company's power and energy division, which supplies reciprocating engines and generator sets to AI data centers, has become a major growth driver. With management projecting sales to triple between 2024-2030 and a 32-year streak of dividend increases, analysts believe the stock has further upside potential.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong 184% stock price appreciation driven by AI data center demand for power solutions. Management raised guidance with projected sales tripling by 2030. 32-year dividend increase streak demonstrates financial strength and shareholder commitment. Analyst expects further gains ahead.
Recently announced major AI data center partnership with Chevron in Texas, indicating significant investment in AI infrastructure expansion and reliance on Caterpillar's power solutions.
Mentioned as partner in major AI data center project with Microsoft, but the article focuses on Caterpillar's role rather than Chevron's strategic positioning or financial outlook.
Identified as fellow industrial company providing power infrastructure alongside Caterpillar, but receives minimal coverage and no specific analysis of its prospects.

title: Chevron is Building a Massive Power Plant to Support a Microsoft AI Data Center. Here's What it Means for the Energy Stock.
author: Matt Dilallo
description: Chevron has signed a 20-year power agreement with Microsoft to build a 2.67 GW natural gas-fired power plant in Texas called Project Kilby to support Microsoft's AI data center. The $7 billion project represents a new growth platform for Chevron, leveraging its Permian Basin gas resources to generate predictable cash flows and reduce commodity price volatility, with power delivery expected to begin in 2028.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Chevron benefits from a 20-year stable revenue contract, a new growth platform in power generation, predictable cash flows reducing commodity volatility, expected mid-teens returns on investment, and potential for multiple future projects as AI data center demand grows.

Microsoft secures reliable, dedicated power supply for its AI data center expansion needs, reduces strain on regional power grids through co-location, and supports its AI growth strategy with long-term energy certainty.
GE Vernova will supply most of the gas turbines and electrical infrastructure for the project, representing a significant equipment supply contract.
Caterpillar's Solar Turbines subsidiary will provide additional turbine capacity for the power plant project.

title: 2 Brilliant Industrial Stocks to Buy for the AI Spending Boom
author: Eric Volkman
description: The article highlights two industrial companies positioned to profit from massive AI data center infrastructure spending: Caterpillar, which supplies power generation equipment and gensets for data centers, and Vertiv Holdings, a specialized manufacturer of liquid-cooling and critical-power solutions. Both companies are experiencing strong revenue growth and raising guidance, with Caterpillar tripling its 2030 power generation sales forecast and Vertiv posting 30% year-over-year sales growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong revenue growth (22% overall, 21% in power and energy division), dramatically raised 2030 power generation sales forecast from doubling to tripling 2024 levels, positioned as crucial supplier for AI data center infrastructure

Exceptional growth metrics with 30% year-over-year sales growth, free cash flow up 147%, net income more than doubled, consistently raising guidance, well-positioned in both retrofit and greenfield data center projects with strong addressable market
